tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria). These findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with metabolic derangements; diabetic ketoacidosis is unlikely given low glucose/HbA1c. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
